sources.excerpt: Retail managers and franchise operators know the hassle of updating paper posters across multiple locations. For frequently changing content — such as class schedules, seasonal notices or limited-time promotions — paper posters may be simple to place, but the repeated time and cost of printing and replacing them can quickly add up. title: Samsung Study Highlights Rising Privacy Concerns in Public Smartphone Use
summary: A new Samsung Electronics study reveals widespread exposure of personal content during public smartphone use across Europe. Surveying 11,000 people, the research found that 56% have accidentally viewe

sources.title: Samsung Study Highlights Rising Privacy Concerns in Public Smartphone Use
sources.excerpt: A new Samsung Electronics study reveals widespread exposure of personal content during public smartphone use across Europe. Surveying 11,000 people, the research found that 56% have accidentally viewed someone else’s screen, while 24% admitted doing so out of curiosity. The findings highlight a gap between perceived and actual privacy. While 48% believe their phone use remains private in public, 52% say it is easy to see others’ screens. sources.excerpt: For decades, TV has been the most familiar screen in daily life. From shared living room viewing in the analog era to today’s personalized, multi-device experience, TV has continually evolved — redefining how we watch. At the center of this shift is the connected TV market. Unlike traditional TV, where viewers follow fixed programming schedules, connected TVs allow users to discover and enjoy content in a more personalized way. title: Nvidia App adds 'Auto Shader Compilation' for faster load times in games — beta feature automatically recompiles shaders in the background after every driver update
summary: The Nvidia App can now automatically recompile shaders for you in the background after every GPU driver update. This should save gamers several minutes across different t

sources.title: Nvidia App adds 'Auto Shader Compilation' for faster load times in games — beta feature automatically recompiles shaders in the background after every driver update
sources.excerpt: The Nvidia App can now automatically recompile shaders for you in the background after every GPU driver update. This should save gamers several minutes across different titles, especially blockbuster ones, where shader compilation can often delay your session. You still need to compile shaders for the first time after a new install, however.
